description: A comprehensive museum showcasing Bashu culture and the history of Chengdu, offering insights into the profound heritage of the Land of Abundance.
A folk culture commercial street where you can experience Western Sichuan customs and traditional snack culture.
The largest comprehensive museum in Sichuan Province, featuring extensive displays of Bashu culture, ethnic relics, and fine art.
A well-known snack hub near the University of Electronic Science and Technology, gathering a variety of Chengdu specialty snacks and night market culture.
A UNESCO World Heritage Site and an outstanding example of ancient hydraulic engineering.
The only temple in China dedicated to both monarchs and their ministers, an important site for showcasing Three Kingdoms culture.
The premier Taoist temple in Western Sichuan, originally built during the Tang Dynasty, known for its rich Taoist culture, exquisite architecture, and thriving incense offerings.
A preserved Qing Dynasty street district, a classic alleyway to experience the slow-paced lifestyle culture of old Chengdu.
One of the birthplaces of Taoism in China, a renowned Taoist mountain famous for its tranquility.
